Everything printed\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-applypatch.sample:# to the output or error channels will be visible to the user.\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-applypatch.sample:#\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-applypatch.sample:# Note that there is a sibling hook called `post-applypatch` (also without further context)\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-applypatch.sample:# which is run after the commit was created. It is useful to use the commit hash for further\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-applypatch.sample:# processing, like sending information to the involved parties.\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-applypatch.sample:# Finally, the `applypatch-msg` hook is called at the very beginning of the `git am` operation\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-applypatch.sample:# to provide access to the commit-message.\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-applypatch.sample:#\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-applypatch.sample:# To enable this hook remove the `.sample` suffix from this file entirely.\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-applypatch.sample:# Retrieve the path to the pre-commit hook script using the \"git rev-parse\" command.\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-applypatch.sample:precommit=\"$(git rev-parse --git-path hooks/pre-commit)\"\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-applypatch.sample:# Check if the pre-commit hook script exists and is executable.\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-applypatch.sample:# If it does, execute it passing the arguments from this script (if any) using the \"exec\" command.\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-applypatch.sample:test -x \"$precommit\" && exec \"$precommit\" ${1+\"$@\"}\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-applypatch.sample:# Be sure to exit without error if `exec` isn't called.\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-applypatch.sample::\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-commit.sample:#!/bin/sh\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-commit.sample:# A sample hook to prevent commits with merge-markers\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-commit.sample:#####################################################\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-commit.sample:# This example hook rejects changes that are about to be committed with merge markers,\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-commit.sample:# as that would be a clear indication of a failed merge. It is triggered by `git commit`\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-commit.sample:# and returning with non-zero exit status prevents the commit from being created.\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-commit.sample:#\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-commit.sample:# To enable this hook remove the `.sample` suffix from this file entirely.\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-commit.sample:# Check for merge markers in modified files\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-commit.sample:for file in $(git diff --cached --name-only); do\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-commit.sample:    if grep -q -E '^(<<<<<<<|=======|>>>>>>>|\\|\\|\\|\\|\\|\\|\\|)$' \"$file\"; then\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-commit.sample:        echo \"Error: File '$file' contains merge markers. Please remove them before committing.\"\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-commit.sample:        exit 1\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-commit.sample:    fi\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-commit.sample:done\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-commit.sample:# Exit with success if there are no errors\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-commit.sample:exit 0\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-merge-commit.sample:#!/bin/sh\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-merge-commit.sample:# A sample hook to check commits created by `git merge`\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-merge-commit.sample:#######################################################\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-merge-commit.sample:#\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-merge-commit.sample:# This hook is invoked by `git merge` without further context right before creating a commit.\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-merge-commit.sample:# It should be used to validate the current state that is supposed to be committed, or exit\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-merge-commit.sample:# with a non-zero status to prevent the commit.\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-merge-commit.sample:# All output will be visible to the user.\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-merge-commit.sample:#\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-merge-commit.sample:# To enable this hook remove the `.sample` suffix from this file entirely.\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-merge-commit.sample:# Check if the pre-commit hook exists and is executable. If it is, it executes the pre-commit hook script.\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-merge-commit.sample:test -x \"$GIT_DIR/hooks/pre-commit\" && exec \"$GIT_DIR/hooks/pre-commit\"\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-merge-commit.sample:# Be sure to exit without error if `exec` isn't called.\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-merge-commit.sample::\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-push.sample:#!/bin/sh\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-push.sample:# Check for \"DELME\" in commit messages of about-to-be-pushed commits\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-push.sample:####################################################################\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-push.sample:# This hook script is triggered by `git push` right after a connection to the remote\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-push.sample:# was established and its initial response was received, and right before generating\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-push.sample:# and pushing a pack-file.\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-push.sample:# The operation will be aborted when exiting with a non-zero status.\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-push.sample:#\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-push.sample:# The following arguments are provided:\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-push.sample:#\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-push.sample:# $1 - The symbolic name of the remote to push to, like \"origin\" or the URL like \"https://github.com/GitoxideLabs/gitoxide\" if there is no such name.\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-push.sample:# $2 - The URL of the remote to push to, like \"https://github.com/GitoxideLabs/gitoxide\".\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-push.sample:#\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-push.sample:# The hook should then read from standard input in a line-by-line fashion and split the following space-separated fields:\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-push.sample:#\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-push.sample:# * local ref - the left side of a ref-spec, i.e. \"local\" of the \"local:refs/heads/remote\" ref-spec\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-push.sample:# * local hash - the hash of the commit pointed to by `local ref`\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-push.sample:# * remote ref - the right side of a ref-spec, i.e. \"refs/heads/remote\" of the \"local:refs/heads/remote\" ref-spec\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-push.sample:# * remote hash - the hash of the commit pointed to by `remote ref`\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-push.sample:#\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-push.sample:# In this example, we abort the push if any of the about-to-be-pushed commits have \"DELME\" in their commit message.\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-push.sample:#\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-push.sample:# To enable this hook remove the `.sample` suffix from this file entirely.\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-push.sample:remote=\"$1\"\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-push.sample:url=\"$2\"\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-push.sample:# Check each commit being pushed\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-push.sample:while read _local_ref local_hash _remote_ref _remote_hash; do\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-push.sample:  # Skip if the local hash is all zeroes (deletion)\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-push.sample:  zero_sha=$(printf \"%0${#local_hash}d\" 0)\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-push.sample:  if [ \"$local_hash\" = \"$zero_sha\" ]; then\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-push.sample:    continue\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-push.sample:  fi\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-push.sample:  # Get the commit message\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-push.sample:  commit_msg=$(git log --format=%s -n 1 \"$local_hash\")\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-push.sample:  # Check if the commit message contains \"DELME\"\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-push.sample:  if echo \"$commit_msg\" | grep -iq \"DELME\"; then\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-push.sample:    echo \"Error: Found commit with 'DELME' in message. Push aborted to $remote ($url) aborted.\" 1>&2\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-push.sample:    exit 1\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-push.sample:  fi\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-push.sample:done\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-push.sample:# If no commit with \"DELME\" found, allow the push\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-push.sample:exit 0\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:#!/bin/sh\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:# A sample hook to validate the branches involved in a rebase operation\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:#######################################################################\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:#\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:# This hook is invoked right before `git rebase` starts its work and\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:# prevents anything else to happen by returning a non-zero exit code.\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:#\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:# The following arguments are provided:\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:#\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:# $1 - the branch that contains the commit from which $2 was forked.\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:# $2 - the branch being rebased or no second argument at all if the rebase applies to `HEAD`.\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:#\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:# This example hook aborts the rebase operation if the branch being rebased is not up to date\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:# with the latest changes from the upstream branch, or if there are any uncommitted changes.\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:#\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:# To enable this hook remove the `.sample` suffix from this file entirely.\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:upstream_branch=$1\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:if [ \"$#\" -eq 2 ]; then\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:  branch_being_rebased=$2\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:else\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:  branch_being_rebased=$(git symbolic-ref --quiet --short HEAD) || exit 0 # ignore rebases on detached heads\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:fi\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:# Check if the branch being rebased is behind the upstream branch\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:if git log --oneline ${upstream_branch}..${branch_being_rebased} > /dev/null; then\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:  echo \"Warning: The branch being rebased (${branch_being_rebased}) is behind the upstream branch (${upstream_branch}).\" 1>&2\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:  echo \"Please update your branch before rebasing.\" 1>&2\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:  exit 1\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:fi\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:# Check if there are any uncommitted changes\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:if ! git diff-index --quiet HEAD --; then\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:  echo \"Warning: There are uncommitted changes in your branch ${branch_being_rebased}.\" 1>&2\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:  echo \"Please commit or stash your changes before rebasing.\" 1>&2\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:  exit 2\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:fi\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:# All good, let the rebase proceed.\r\n./.git/hooks/pre-rebase.sample:exit 0\r\n./.git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg.sample:#!/bin/sh\r\n./.git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg.sample:# A hook called by `git commit` to adjust the commit message right before the user sees it\r\n./.git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg.sample:##########################################################################################\r\n./.git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg.sample:#\r\n./.git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg.sample:# This script is called by `git commit` after commit message was initialized and right before\r\n./.git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg.sample:# an editor is launched.\r\n./.git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg.sample:#\r\n./.git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg.sample:# It receives one to three arguments:\r\n./.git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg.sample:#\r\n./.git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg.sample:# $1 - the path to the file containing the commit message. It can be edited to change the message.\r\n./.git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg.sample:# $2 - the kind of source of the message contained in $1. Possible values are\r\n./.git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg.sample:#      \"message\" - a message was provided via `-m` or `-F`\r\n./.git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg.sample:#      \"commit\" - `-c`, `-C` or `--amend` was given\r\n./.git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg.sample:#      \"squash\" - the `.git/SQUASH_MSG` file exists\r\n./.git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg.sample:#      \"merge\" - this is a merge or the `.git/MERGE` file exists\r\n./.git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg.sample:#      \"template\" - `-t` was provided or `commit.template` was set\r\n./.git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg.sample:# $3 - If $2 is \"commit\" then this is the hash of the commit.\r\n./.git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg.sample:#      It can also take other values, best understood by studying the source code at\r\n./.git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg.sample:#      https://github.com/git/git/blob/aa9166bcc0ba654fc21f198a30647ec087f733ed/builtin/commit.c#L745\r\n./.git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg.sample:#\r\n./.git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg.sample:# The following example\r\n./.git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg.sample:#\r\n./.git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg.sample:# To enable this hook remove the `.sample` suffix from this file entirely.\r\n./.git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg.sample:COMMIT_MSG_FILE=$1\r\n./.git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg.sample:# Check if the commit message file is empty or already contains a message\r\n./.git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg.sample:if [ -s \"$COMMIT_MSG_FILE\" ]; then\r\n./.git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg.sample:  # If the commit message is already provided, exit without making any changes.\r\n./.git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg.sample:  # This can happen if the user provided a message via `-m` or a template.\r\n./.git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg.sample:  exit 0\r\n./.git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg.sample:fi\r\n./.git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg.sample:# Retrieve the branch name from the current HEAD commit\r\n./.git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg.sample:BRANCH_NAME=$(git symbolic-ref --short HEAD)\r\n./.git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg.sample:# Generate a default commit message based on the branch name\r\n./.git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg.sample:DEFAULT_MSG=\"\"\r\n./.git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg.sample:case \"$BRANCH_NAME\" in\r\n./.git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg.sample:  \"feature/*\")\r\n./.git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg.sample:    DEFAULT_MSG=\"feat: \"\r\n./.git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg.sample:    ;;\r\n./.git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg.sample:  \"bugfix/*\")\r\n./.git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg.sample:    DEFAULT_MSG=\"fix: \"\r\n./.git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg.sample:    ;;\r\n./.git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg.sample:  *)\r\n./.git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg.sample:    DEFAULT_MSG=\"chore: \"\r\n./.git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg.sample:    ;;\r\n./.git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg.sample:esac\r\n./.git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg.sample:# Set the commit message that will be presented to the user.\r\n./.git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg.sample:echo \"$DEFAULT_MSG\" > \"$COMMIT_MSG_FILE\"\r\n./.git/info/exclude:# This file contains repository-wide exclude patterns that git will ignore.\r\n./.git/info/exclude:# They are local and will not be shared when pushing or pulling.\r\n./.git/info/exclude:# When using Rust the following would be typical exclude patterns.\r\n./.git/info/exclude:# Remove the '# ' prefix to let them take
